name: Bloom Filter
def: A space-efficient probabilistic set with no false negatives but tunable false positives.
problem: Test set membership over huge key sets using a tiny, fixed memory budget.
mechanism: k hash functions set k bits in a bit array per insert; a query is "maybe present" only if all k bits are set. No deletion in the basic form.
benefits: O(k) insert/query independent of set size
Great as a pre-filter to avoid expensive lookups
failures: Mismatched hash count k vs size m
whenNot: Small sets where a hash set is cheaper
myths: "It can return false negatives" — never; only false positives
